reviews.title: TRANSPORTATION IS NOT CONVENIENT
reviews.text: Road to hotel is terrible, dangerous. You need to pay double taxi fare to go down city.  Schedule of shuttle bus is super strange, check out time is 12:00 but they only have shuttle bus in 5:00pm, it means you need to wait 5 hours more if you want use their shuttle bus service. If not, you need to pay double fare by yourself for taxi. Schedule was changed but they didn't update on agoda.  Hotel has good view.
reviews.response:
Dear Tuyen,
I highly appreciate your stay and comments.
Please accept my apologies for any inconveniences regarding transportation. For your kind information, the government is upgrading the road and it will be finished in Nov 2018.
Hope we have another opportunity to welcome you back with better customers services!
Best regards,
Phạm Minh Tuấn
Hotel Manager

Wish to report that I have closely verified all the points with our related Service Departments. As a result of our checking with scrutiny, I found that the house was full while you sent the requests. However, some guests changed the arrival dates. Therefore, booking via Agoda was available after a few days after your request via email. Please rest assured that I will ask my team to update or get back to you when rooms are available from now on. 

Thank you once again for taking time to share your valuable feedback. I eagerly await another opportunity of supporting you while sending the request in the future. Please do email me at hm.nuisam@victoriahotels.asia if I am of any assistance in any bookings.

Best regards,

Pham Minh Tuan
Hotel Manager

reviews.title: Relaxing environment with incredible views
reviews.positives: The view is fantastic from the top of the mountain, especially at sunrise and sunset. Each room is like a little bungalow with your own patio to sit out on. Chau Doc was the highlight of my entire trip to Vietnam, in part because staying Victoria was so nice, and I wish I spent more time there. Definitely visit the Hang Pagoda! Dinner was also tasty with big portions.

reviews.title: Muy buen sitio para desconectar
reviews.positives: El hotel es realmente precioso: tiene un estilo rústico con muchísimo encanto, las vistas son absolutamente espectaculares y te dejan sin aliento desde cualquier punto (la habitación, la piscina o el restaurante), sobre todo cuando los arrozales están verdes. La habitación es muy amplia y cuenta con una terraza igual de grande, lo que lo hace aún más especial. Además, todo está impecablemente limpio, no hay ninguna queja en ese sentido. El personal es otro punto fuerte: muy amable, educado y siempre con buena actitud. Y, por último, la tranquilidad es total; al estar en lo alto de la montaña no se escucha absolutamente nada, lo que lo convierte en un lugar perfecto para desconectar
reviews.negatives: El precio es excesivo para tratarse de un hotel de tres estrellas. La cama no resulta especialmente cómoda y las almohadas son demasiado altas, incluso para alguien acostumbrado a dormir con altura, lo que dificulta el descanso. La piscina, aunque visualmente atractiva, no está bien pensada: el nivel del agua es bajo en relación con el borde, por lo que no puedes quedarte relajado disfrutando de las vistas desde dentro. La zona poco profunda es demasiado baja (unos 80 cm) y la profunda pasa directamente a unos 1,85 m con una pendiente muy pronunciada, lo que la hace poco práctica para disfrutarla con calma. Al final, es una piscina más para nadar y salir que para relajarse, y se echa en falta un diseño tipo infinity pool que permita disfrutar realmente del entorno desde el agua

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
Our vacation was VN holidays and we stayed here for 2 nights. Actually our group of families stay in Victoria Nui Sam 2 times/ year, but for this time, we have not satisfied during our staying, by the below noted:
1. The noise of the northern guests group was over that broke the peace, calm of VNS's environment. It was also complained to the service team, but there is no any solution to minimize or reduce it. Hence, other guests had to suffer it.  
2. VNS, has opened to outside visitors for café, swimming pool. There original style has been affected. 
I do hope, VNS team may have some solution to keep its calm, peace which is one of the strongest points of the lodge. 
Over all, my family and I will come back next time. Thank you
reviews.response:
Dear Mr. Minh, 
Thank you for your feedback!
It is truly disappointing to know your stay was not up to your expectations in noise of in-house groups and outside visitors …, for which I sincerely offer you my deepest apology. I appreciate your feedback on these areas for improvement which will be shared in earnest with our team members to improve right away to best serve our guests.

Thank you once again for taking time to share your valuable feedback. I eagerly await another opportunity of hosting you and your family in the near future. Please do email me at hm.nuisam@victoriahotels.asia if I am of any assistance in directly booking your return stay.

Best regards,

Pham Minh Tuan
Hotel Manager

reviews.title: Paysages magnifiques , hôtel décevant
reviews.positives: L' Hôtel est situé en dehors de la ville avec une vue dominante sur les rizières incroyable. La piscine est idéalement placée pour permettre justement cette très belle vue. La location de scooter sur place qui permet d'aller découvrir les environs ( forêt de Trasu et autres ).Le petit déjeuner buffet.
reviews.negatives:
Pour des raisons pratiques, nous avons décidé de diner à l'hôtel. Grosse erreur car nous n'avons pas pu profiter de notre repas en raison d'une invasion de moucherons et moustiques à l'intérieur comme à l'extérieur. Il semble que ce soit coutumier car le personnel n'était pas du tout surpris. Bref, repas chèrement payé gâché.
Pour un établissement de ce rang, les abords de la très piscine sont sales, dommage.
Les chambres sont spacieuses avec une belle terrasse mais mériteraient une remise au goût du jour. Le rapport qualité / prix n'est pas bon mais dans ce coin là, mais les environs sont tellement beaux !

reviews.text:
Trip during 12-13-14 July: stayed at Rocky Hill (Room 242) and Sunrise (Room 173). 
*** Wifi in both rooms and restaurant area are non-accessible though the signal bar looks strong
*** There is no gym (don't expect that. But there is a healthy trail you can walk around)
*** Room 173 (beautiful view) has a clogged sink. 
Overally, this choice is bang for the buck. The views from the room and restaurant are breaktaking. You can see Cambodia when looking west. 
Wifi needs huge huge improvement!!!

reviews.title: Holiday with family
reviews.text:
- Spacious area, suitable for relaxation.
- The security staff and vehicle coordinators were very enthusiastic in arranging parking spots, even running out in the rain to direct cars to the right places.
- We were not satisfied as our family had booked on Booking two months in advance for a room with two single beds, suitable for an elderly person who has trouble sleeping. However, upon arrival, we were given a room with one single bed and assigned a room close to the pool, which made it difficult and tiring to go up and down to the room.
- Drinking water was only provided in one glass bottle for three people each day.
- The room did not have a vanity table, which is inconvenient for women.
- We hope that in the future, when booking through Booking, the reservation will be honored as confirmed.
- If I had known prior to checking in that it was a single bed, I would have canceled right away and chosen another hotel that was more convenient in terms of access and beds suitable for the family.

reviews.title: incredible views
reviews.text: I have stayed at Victoria Cantho 4 times and Victoria Chau Doc once and absolutely loved them.  This one is completely different and i have to say i was disappointed, however, it was also a lot cheaper.  this hotel is not for people with mobility problems, lots of steps with no railings.  it is actually a very strange hotel, built years ago but not opened until last year.  reception area is terrific with magnificent views.  our room also had magnificent views from the large verandah, but the strange thing was that there was no furniture out there and no umbrella/shade so we could only utilise it on dusk and had to carry our inside chairs out there.  Sub-standard for such a fantastic chain of hotels.  the free shuttle is good and necessary - don't know how you would get a taxi from there as it is way up on top of a mountain.  the pool is nice and enjoys the same fabulous views.  I would recommend it but only for the view.

reviews.title: Awsome view, nice staff but quite inconvenient transportation
reviews.text:
I was there last week for sudden trip with my friend. I heard about this hotel before coming to and be impressed with its location (on the middle of mountain). Honestly, I was pleasant with its view (especially in the sunset), staff. However, I think hotel management should be improved more as the followings:
1. Add more timetable of shuttle bus for transportation from mountain to downtown. I had to use Xe Om or Taxi with high cost for moving from the hotel in the evening.
2. Breakfast should be more diversity. I supposed that there were not many people stay there this season so I could only find bacon, egg, bread for breakfast. It is better for having dried noodle, steamed rice or more traditional dishes in Chau Doc (such as Bun Ca (Fish Rice Noodle), very famous and delicious).
3. Facilities in superior room should be changed or fixed. Toilet shower is old and water is flooded in the floor. 

Finally, I felt comfortable for 2 days in Victoria Nui Sam Lodge and I will take my family to stay again in the near future.
Thao

reviews.title: Spectacular view
reviews.text:
Stunning view from uphill looking down to rice paddies was the greatest impression i had here. Nice design of rooms lying on mountain side give the best view of sunrise or sunset depends on room's direction, but it's not suitable for old people or family with infant since you have to climb up and down many steps. Decorations in room is a bit simple but it matches with Mekong Delta spirit. I have stayed at many premises of Victoria and so far I have been pleased with their service thoroughly. Attentive staff, fast check in/out, wide spread of breakfast, strong wifi in rooms and public area are their standards as it should be. 

The only minus is too many mosquitoes in the room though there is net for bed but it couldn't help. Please figure out the way to avoid this, e.g. by insecticide spray or lemongrass essential oil.

reviews.title: Great hotel but not my taste
reviews.text:
The hotel is great, location is beautiful for sunset and sunrise.
I didn't sleep well because of unconfortable pillow. It may not be the case of every room tho.
Also, I'm sure that if I'd have asked to change the pillow, the staff would have done it. It was late so I didn't call them.

Breakfast: They had several vietnamese options but overall it was too much international for what I was looking for.

To sum up :
If you are a westerner and want to visit Vietnam without feeling far from home, this is a good place. You will have a lovely Time.
During my stay, most customers were french, german and english.

If you want an authentic experience, look for a homestay in town. That's what I did the day before staying at the Victoria and it was 100 times better.

reviews.title: Worth staying
reviews.text:
Pros:
- Nice place on the mountain, with beautiful view to the rice field, mountain and river over far away. Swimming pool is small, yet with very special view. Spacious and nice balcony.
- Nice staff, great resort manager. He offered us a free stay after check-out until 4pm.
- Surrounding environment is very nice. We went there in September and Tigon flowers are blooming everywhere on the way from mountain foot to the resort, very romantic.

Cons:
- Mosquitos:  although the resort said they have frequent pest control, it seems to be ineffective to prevent mosquitos.  We cannot stay in the balcony at night because of mosquitos and have to close the door, which is a pity.
- If you want to go somewhere near the resort, u can rent a motorbike (can ask the receptionist) since taxi will has surcharge for climbing up the mountain. However motorbike rent is very high here, which is double the price in other province. Tourism in Chau Doc is not professional yet. But this is not the fault of the resort themselves.

reviews.text:
- The hotel has a very beautiful view, the facilities are convenient, and when I arrived, the staff welcomed me very well and were enthusiastic.
- Regarding the reception staff, they need to reassess. When customers call to make a reservation, they cannot check the room availability right away and have to ask for the phone number again so that the reservation department can contact later.
The next day I booked through Agoda and called to check the reservation, but the staff still could not check the booking and again asked for the phone number to verify and respond later.
I then received feedback that there was a reservation, and I made two additional requests:
First: set up a honeymoon,
Second: note that another person will check in under a different name. You received all the information and confirmed it was noted, but my friend went to the room with the booking and the reception couldn’t find it? The room was not set up for the honeymoon?
- Victoria is a well-known system, and the service quality is very good; almost no hotel in An Giang can compare to it, but the staff is unprofessional. Customers call in but cannot check room status? Cannot verify bookings? After receiving customer notes, they do not note them down.

reviews.title: Exceptional
reviews.text:
The lodge offers awesome view of the rice fields. The restaurant La Giang offers a very decent menu of Vietnamese and Western food. We were not very impressed by the variety of breakfast buffet but it did offer a few choices of Vietnamese food (noodle, congee and rice crepe) and basic Western fare (bread, ham, jam, egg) and some fruit/ sweet desserts. 

The pool is small, best for photo ops and admiring the view from above - so don't expect a serious work out. Rooms (we stayed in the 28sqm ones) are clean and decent. Remember to close the doors properly as at night there're A LOT of small wasps trying to get in. 

It's a very good stop over if you're exploring the region or on the way to Cambodia. 

For excursion, we strongly recommend visiting Tra Su forest (30 min away from Lodge) which offers combination of bike (xe om), boat ride and paddle boat with lovely guides and then lunch (good fresh food cooked on the spot) - a surprisingly efficient operation and very clean environment that's hard to come by in Vietnam. If you go, remember to wear hats & sunscreen and mosquito spray.

reviews.text:
We had a lovely stay at Victoria Hotel in the Nui Sam area of Vietnam. It was a truly enjoyable experience, made even more special by the spectacular views surrounding the hotel. The rooms and facilities were very clean, comfortable, and well maintained, making it a relaxing stay for our family.

Breakfast was a highlight, especially the Bún Bò Huế, which was absolutely delicious and a wonderful way to start the day.

However, I would not recommend this hotel for elderly travellers, as there are many stairs and it can be quite tiring to walk up and down to access the rooms and the pool. Overall, it was a memorable stay with beautiful scenery and great food.

reviews.title: I love this place!
reviews.text:
I love landscape photography so you can’t beat this place for the views. You can see both the sunset and sunrise from the lobby/restaurant level!  The only downside to this hotel is that there are no elevators. The stairs are very steep, so if you struggle with that I recommend requesting a room closest to the lobby level. But then it’s a long walk down to the pool. So just be aware before booking. The restaurant is open air so is probably very hot during the very hottest months. I stayed Dec 8-11. It was 90F during my stay but I felt fine. The staff is very helpful placing fans near you. The other thing to note is there are a lot of mosquitoes during the evening but I didn’t have issues with the help of mosquito repellant. 

The staff was the best part of my stay. They were all very nice, courteous, and helpful. 

Breakfast was very good. I was pleasantly surprised to find they make very good croissants!

I highly recommend and would stay again.

reviews.title: The best (or worst) location depending on your style
reviews.text: This is a spectacular hotel near the top of a mountain, overlooking the whole (green) valley. There is not much immediately around.the hotel. If you have a car and look for a secluded place to relax, this is the best in town, I didn't check but I guess they would have a shuttle of some kind to take you to town which is only about 10, 15 minutes away. We had a car and a driver so it wasn't a problem. The Staff, as often found in Vietnam, were extremely friendly, helpful and respectful. I want to stress the last point, respectfulness, as a differentiator about the service in Vietnam to compare with the usual service you often find in America or Europe.They may be friendly but would never treat you like a King you "deserve" to be treated, simply because it is not in their DNA to respect customers.

reviews.title: A wonderful place for nature lovers.
reviews.text: The hotel is located on Mount Sam, and since I went on a Tuesday, it wasn't as crowded as on the weekends. The staff is friendly and lovely, from the security guards in the parking lot to the receptionists and the restaurant staff. If you are traveling with a family group of 6, you can book 2 adjacent Family rooms (with a connecting door), which is very reasonable. This family area is close to the pool, but the downside is that it is located on the lowest floor of the hotel, so going up and down the stairs can be a bit tiring; however, the staff will help carry your luggage. The view is unbeatable, and after returning, you'll have a lot of photos for social media. The room is clean, and the hotel provides mosquito nets and mosquito repellent, although there were no mosquitoes in the room. There are also 2 hand sanitizer dispensers in front of the room, which is something I notice is rarely provided in accommodations these days. Despite the pandemic being over, my family and I still frequently use hand sanitizer after being outdoors.
reviews.response:
Kính gửi anh Tiến,
Cảm ơn anh rất nhiều vì đã cho khách sạn chúng tôi một đánh giá hoàn hảo như vậy. Phục vụ anh cũng là niềm vui của chúng tôi. Tất cả nhân viên khách sạn đều mong được chào đón anh trở lại. Xin vui lòng liên hệ với chúng tôi để được hỗ trợ nếu anh cần.
Trân trọng,
Mỹ Phướng
Tiếp tân

reviews.text: Having spent 3 nights below in Chau Doc at a hotel that just didn't feel right, we spent our final day in the area at Victoria. What a difference! Big terrace outside room with the lovely views across rice fields to distant mountain range and nice food! Room was good with v comfortable bed and bedding. Shower good - just needs the grouting tidying up. A lovely setting and we were so lucky to enjoy our first peaceful,  relaxing afternoon of our 6 week trip at the pool - all quiet and to ourselves until a tour group arrived to also enjoy it. reviews.text: Mọi thứ đều tốt
reviews.response:
Xin chào chị Thuy,

Cảm ơn chị đã tin tưởng chọn lựa khách sạn Victoria Núi Sam cho kỳ nghỉ của mình. Khách sạn và toàn thể nhân viên rất cảm kích về những đánh giá cao của chị về chất lượng dịch vụ của khách sạn. Chúng tôi rất vui vì sự cố gắng nỗ lực của chúng tôi đã mang đến sự hài lòng cho chị và gia đình. Hy vọng rằng, với những ấn tượng tốt đẹp ấy chúng tôi sẽ vinh dự được chào đón chị một lần nữa trong thời gian tới.

Chúc chị và gia đình sức khỏe và thành công.
Trân trọng,
Nhi Nguyễn
Bộ phận Lễ Tân

reviews.title: Great location with spacious view
reviews.text: This was first time I stayed 2 days vacation at Victoria Nui Sam Lodge Chau Doc. The hotel is located on the top of Sam mountain with a wide and sweeping view in Chau Doc, especially can see the Cambodia-Vietnam border. Spacious room with a private balcony so it's very comfortable. There is an infinity pool and La Giang restaurant serving breakfast buffet with many delicious and diverse dishes of the Mekong delta.
